articles.headline: RBI Board Approves Record ₹2.11 Lakh Crore Dividend to Union Government
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has approved a record surplus transfer of ₹2,10,874 crore to the central government for fiscal year 2023-24. This amount is more than double the previous year’s transfer and significantly exceeds the government's budget estimate of ₹1.02 lakh crore. The move is expected to provide a major fiscal boost and help reduce the national deficit.

articles.headline: Indian Stock Markets Hit Record Highs Following RBI Dividend News
articles.summary: The Sensex and Nifty touched all-time highs on Wednesday as investors reacted to the RBI’s announcement of a record dividend. The Sensex surged nearly 1,200 points to cross the 75,000 mark during intraday trade. Banking and financial stocks led the rally, with market participants expecting lower government borrowing and improved liquidity in the financial system.

articles.headline: Adani Enterprises to Consider Raising Funds via Share Sale on May 28
articles.summary: Adani Enterprises announced its board will meet on May 28 to evaluate a proposal for raising funds through the issuance of equity shares. Sources suggest the group aims to raise between $2 billion and $3 billion. The capital will likely be used for debt reduction and funding green energy projects, following a similar fundraising plan by Adani Energy Solutions.

articles.headline: IndiGo Reports Record Quarterly Profit of ₹1,895 Crore in Q4
articles.summary: InterGlobe Aviation (IndiGo) reported a net profit of ₹1,895 crore for the March quarter, marking its highest-ever quarterly profit. The airline’s revenue rose by 27% year-on-year to ₹68,904 crore. The results were driven by robust passenger demand and higher yields. Despite grounded aircraft issues, the carrier remains optimistic about its international expansion plans.

articles.headline: Severe Heatwave Grips Northern India as Temperatures Cross 47 Degrees Celsius
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department has issued a red alert for Delhi, Rajasthan, Punjab, and Haryana as a severe heatwave persists. Najafgarh recorded a peak temperature of 47.4°C. The heatwave is expected to continue for the next five days, straining the power grid and impacting agricultural operations across the northern and western regions of the country.

articles.headline: Enforcement Directorate Investigates Money Laundering in Pune Porsche Crash Case
articles.summary: The ED is probing a potential money laundering angle in the Pune Porsche car crash involving a minor from a prominent builder family. The agency is looking into allegations of bribery used to swap blood samples and influence officials. Local police have arrested several individuals, including the minor's father and grandfather, as public outrage over the incident grows.

articles.headline: Global Investors Await Nvidia Earnings and US Federal Reserve Minutes
articles.summary: International markets remained cautious ahead of Nvidia’s quarterly earnings report, which is seen as a crucial indicator for the global AI sector. Additionally, the release of the US Federal Reserve’s latest meeting minutes is expected to provide clarity on the timeline for interest rate cuts. Most Asian markets traded flat or slightly lower in anticipation of these events.

articles.headline: Election Commission Issues Notices for Model Code Violations in Phase 6
articles.summary: As India prepares for the sixth phase of polling, the Election Commission has issued fresh notices to political leaders over communal remarks and personal attacks. The commission warned parties against using religious symbols for campaigning. Over 58 seats across eight states and UTs are scheduled to go to polls, with security being tightened in sensitive zones.

articles.headline: Government Extends Sugar Export Restrictions to Ensure Domestic Price Stability
articles.summary: The Union government has extended the current restrictions on sugar exports beyond May 31 to maintain domestic availability and control retail prices. India, the world’s second-largest sugar producer, faced erratic monsoons that impacted crop yields in Maharashtra and Karnataka. The move aims to prevent inflation ahead of the upcoming festive season.

articles.headline: SEBI Proposes Mandatory Quarterly Reporting for All Listed Debt Entities
articles.summary: Market regulator SEBI has proposed a new framework requiring all entities with listed debt securities to disclose their financial results on a quarterly basis. Currently, such entities are only required to report on a half-yearly basis. The proposal aims to bring debt-listed firms on par with equity-listed companies and increase transparency for bond investors.

articles.headline: Strong Occupancy in Kashmir Hotels Due to Better Road and Rail Links
articles.summary: Kashmir is seeing a surge in tourist arrivals this summer, driven by enhanced connectivity and the Vande Bharat Express. While high-end hotel demand has softened slightly with rates around 5,000 rupees per night, Srinagar is facing significant traffic congestion. Many visitors are now arriving in personal vehicles from Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, and Gujarat, extending stays from religious sites.

articles.headline: Indian Executives Enroll in Generative AI Courses to Future-Proof Careers
articles.summary: Upskilling platforms like Coursera and upGrad report a massive surge in AI-related enrollments as professionals scramble to stay relevant. GenAI and agentic AI courses now account for over 60% of revenue for some providers. The trend is led by mid-career managers and senior executives with 5-15 years of experience, focusing on applying AI to leadership, healthcare, and financial strategy.

articles.headline: China Challenges France as Leading Global Producer of Foie Gras
articles.summary: Chinese farmers are ramping up foie gras production, with output reaching 14,000 tonnes last year. Lower costs and increasing global demand are driving local producers to target overseas markets, including Europe and Southeast Asia. While France remains the top producer, China's rapid growth and affordable pricing are turning the culinary icon into a significant global trade battleground.

articles.headline: India-Japan Summit to Focus on Semiconductor and Critical Mineral Security
articles.summary: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi will lead a delegation of 50 Japanese business leaders, including executives from Suzuki and Toyota, to Guwahati for an annual summit. The talks will prioritize economic security, focusing on semiconductor supply chains, critical minerals, and trusted telecom networks. The event marks the first time the summit is being held in India's Northeast region.

articles.headline: India Remains on Track for One Trillion Dollar Export Target: Goyal
articles.summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal expressed confidence that India will achieve its $1 trillion export target by FY27 despite the West Asia crisis. India has already achieved 15% of the goal. Goyal also noted that a trade framework with the US is finalized but will only be implemented once India secures a tariff advantage over competing nations to ensure a competitive edge for exporters.

articles.headline: Russia Explores Rupee Fund Investments in Indian Ports and SEZs
articles.summary: Moscow is evaluating the use of accumulated rupee funds from trade to invest in India's infrastructure, biotechnology, and agriculture sectors. Key interest lies in the Sagarmala port expansion project and Special Economic Zones. Analysts suggest these investments will help Russia manage its currency surplus while generating long-term returns in India's high-growth economy.

articles.headline: Next-Generation Sequencing Gene Testing Revolutionizes Cancer Diagnosis in India
articles.summary: NGS technology is transforming precision oncology by identifying specific genetic mutations in tumors. The LuNGS Alliance is offering free testing to lung cancer patients, allowing doctors in smaller cities to prescribe targeted therapies instead of standard chemotherapy. While costs are falling, experts call for NGS inclusion in government schemes like Ayushman Bharat to expand access.

articles.headline: PM Modi Attacks TMC and Left Over Decades of Misrule in Bengal
articles.summary: Addressing a rally in Hooghly, PM Narendra Modi claimed the NDA's 'double-engine' government has put West Bengal on a development track after years of corruption by previous regimes. He accused the TMC of 'cut-money' politics and syndicate raj. The PM also highlighted the historical significance of Paschimbanga Divas and promised to focus on industrial growth and security for the state.

articles.headline: AICC and Shashi Tharoor Differ Over Modi’s Response to Sailor Killings
articles.summary: The Congress party and MP Shashi Tharoor presented conflicting views on the Prime Minister's meeting with Donald Trump in France. While the AICC accused Modi of failing to secure an apology for the killing of three Indian sailors in the Gulf of Oman, Tharoor claimed the PM forcefully raised the issue privately. Tharoor noted that the meeting helped warm relations between the two nations.

articles.headline: Manipur to Receive Additional Elite CRPF CoBRA Units for Security
articles.summary: The Union Home Ministry is deploying two more battalions of elite CoBRA units to Manipur to restore normalcy and prevent militant regrouping. This comes amid ongoing ethnic violence that has killed over 260 people since May 2023. Large-scale combing operations are being conducted in conflict-hit areas as the state government seeks to stabilize the region and protect civilians.

articles.headline: Ladakh Bodies Call for Region-Wide Bandh Over Unfulfilled Promises
articles.summary: The Apex Body Leh and Kargil Democratic Alliance have called for a Ladakh-wide bandh on June 23. The organizations accuse the Ministry of Home Affairs of employing delaying tactics and failing to implement commitments made during recent meetings. Protesters are demanding constitutional safeguards and the fulfillment of administrative promises for the Union Territory.

articles.headline: Bihar Government Orders Judicial Inquiry into Controversial Police Encounter
articles.summary: Bihar Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary has ordered a judicial probe by a retired High Court judge into the police encounter of Bharat Tiwari. The incident triggered criticism after Tiwari's family claimed he was killed after surrendering. The inquiry aims to ensure an impartial investigation into the facts of the case, which has drawn scrutiny from both the opposition and ruling NDA allies.

articles.headline: Gujarat Records 680 Dolphins Following Successful Marine Conservation Efforts
articles.summary: A latest survey has recorded over 680 dolphins along Gujarat's coastline, with the highest concentration found in the Gulf of Kutch. Environment Minister Arjun Modhwadia attributed the population recovery to conservation efforts and the participation of local fishing communities. The growth in marine mammals has boosted tourism in areas like Shivrajpur and Poshitra.

articles.headline: Independent Consulting Registrations Grow by 290 Percent as Gig Economy Shifts
articles.summary: India's white-collar gig economy is seeing a structural shift as professionals opt for portfolio careers. Platform Flexing It reports that monthly consultant registrations have crossed 5,000, with 40% of new joiners having 3-8 years of experience. Industrial and manufacturing firms have emerged as the largest consumers of this talent for projects in supply chain, finance, and tech.

articles.headline: Kotak Report Urges New Independence Movement to Reduce Foreign Capital Reliance
articles.summary: A report by Kotak Institutional Equities suggests India needs to reduce its dependence on imported energy, defense equipment, and foreign capital. It warns that geopolitical tensions and protectionism are narrowing India's ability to rely on global imports. The report advocates for stronger domestic manufacturing and policy actions to address vulnerabilities in the services-led growth model.

articles.headline: Generative AI Predicted to Increase Real Estate Sales Velocity by 50 Percent
articles.summary: A report by Credai and EY indicates that GenAI could increase real estate sales by 30-50% by converting leads faster. AI-driven customer intelligence and automated design workflows are expected to reduce land-to-launch cycle times by up to 30%. Early adopters could see cost reductions of 18-20% and significant gains in workforce productivity through intelligence-driven models.

articles.headline: Lack of Purpose-Built Venues Inhibits Growth of India’s Live Concert Economy
articles.summary: Despite high demand for global stars like Travis Scott and Coldplay, India faces a critical lack of dedicated concert arenas. Promoters currently build temporary structures for each show, leading to high production costs (30-40% of revenue) and poor fan experiences. Experts argue that plug-and-play venues are essential to make India a default stop on international world tours.

articles.headline: Europe Swelters Under Heatwave Prompts Crisis Meetings and Health Alerts
articles.summary: A punishing heatwave is sweeping across Europe, with temperatures approaching 41C in France and 38C in Germany. French officials have held emergency meetings as the heat is expected to match historic 2003 highs. The extreme weather is straining tourism in Italy and raising concerns about reduced economic productivity and increased energy use across the continent.

articles.headline: Spanish Judge Bans Prime Minister’s Wife Begona Gomez from Leaving Country
articles.summary: A judge in Madrid has ordered Begona Gomez, wife of Spanish PM Pedro Sanchez, to stand trial for corruption. The judge cited a risk of flight and imposed an exit ban, requiring her to surrender her passport and report to court twice a month. The case is part of several corruption investigations involving the Socialist leader's family that are threatening the minority coalition government.

articles.headline: Ubisoft Co-founder Claude Guillemot Dies in French Plane Crash
articles.summary: Claude Guillemot, who co-founded the French video game giant Ubisoft with his brothers in 1986, has died at age 69. The company confirmed his death following a plane crash in the resort town of La Baule on France's Atlantic coast. Guillemot was also the chairman of Guillemot Corp and played a pivotal role in establishing the company as a major player in the global gaming industry.

articles.headline: Psychological Wellness Trends Identified as Top Hacks for 2026
articles.summary: Wellness trends for 2026 highlight low-cost practices like 'broaden & build' theory to foster resilience and 'time affluence' to prioritize personal rest. Other popular hacks include 'bedtime stacking' for restorative rest and 'micro walks' for physical health. Experts note that these trends represent a cultural shift toward creating healthy boundaries and meaningful social connections.

articles.headline: Retired Physician Art Ulene Training to Climb Mount Kilimanjaro at 89
articles.summary: Art Ulene, a former NBC medical correspondent, is training to summit Mount Kilimanjaro on his 90th birthday. Having started serious climbing at 74, Ulene aims to prove that aging does not necessitate physical decline. He follows a strict training regimen of weightlifting and stair climbing, emphasizing that a long 'healthspan' is more meaningful than simple lifespan.

articles.headline: New Research Recommends Finishing Coffee Nine Hours Before Sleep
articles.summary: A meta-analysis suggests that for optimal rest, individuals should consume their last cup of coffee at least nine hours before bedtime. Caffeine can reduce total sleep time by 45 minutes and increase the time taken to fall asleep. While coffee has health benefits including longevity, its impact on the circadian rhythm varies based on an individual's DNA and metabolism rate.

articles.headline: Brazil Defeats Haiti 3-0 in Meaningful Progress for Ancelotti’s Squad
articles.summary: Brazil secured a 3-0 victory against Haiti, showing signs of tactical renewal under coach Carlo Ancelotti. Goals from Matheus Cunha and Vinicius Jr highlighted a new diamond midfield formation. While the team showed improved fluency compared to their opener, questions remain regarding the fitness of Raphinha and the defensive solidity of Casemiro against stronger opponents.

articles.headline: Toronto Goldfish Swimbappe Makes Accurate World Cup Match Predictions
articles.summary: An oranda goldfish named Swimbappe has become a sensation in Toronto by predicting World Cup match winners from his underwater pitch. Named after Kylian Mbappe, the fish has correctly predicted 14 match outcomes. His tank, designed like a football field, attracts crowds of curious fans who watch the orange creature line up to pick winners every day.

articles.headline: Neeraj Chopra Finishes Fourth in Season-Opening Doha Diamond League Event
articles.summary: Returning from injury, Indian javelin thrower Neeraj Chopra finished fourth in his season opener at the Doha Diamond League with a best throw of 85.69m. Sri Lanka's Rumesh Tharanga Pathirage won the event with an 88.68m effort. Despite the lower finish, Chopra has been named in the 32-member Indian team for the upcoming Glasgow Commonwealth Games.

articles.headline: Australia Women Defeat Netherlands by 98 Runs in T20 World Cup
articles.summary: Australia earned their third consecutive win in the Women's T20 World Cup, reaching a record tournament total of 219-6. Ash Gardner scored 58 while Beth Mooney hit 74 before retiring with a stiff back. The Netherlands were restricted to 121-3 in response. Australia's dominant performance cements their position as favorites to progress further in the tournament.

articles.headline: BJP, Cong trade barbs over Karnataka’s power crisis
articles.summary: The Karnataka government accused the BJP of hoarding coal, exacerbating the state's power crisis. The BJP, in turn, blamed the ruling Congress for poor planning and mismanagement of the energy sector.

articles.headline: RBI wary of inflation, signals tight monetary policy
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India expressed concern over persistent inflation, indicating a continued stance of tight monetary policy. This suggests interest rates may remain elevated to control price pressures.

articles.headline: PM Modi inaugurates new airport in Rajasthan
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated a new international airport in Jodhpur, Rajasthan. The airport is expected to boost tourism and economic activity in the region.

articles.headline: India-US trade talks focus on market access, tariffs
articles.summary: Senior trade officials from India and the United States held discussions on improving market access and resolving tariff disputes. Both nations aim to strengthen bilateral trade ties.

articles.headline: Monsoon likely to be normal this year, says IMD
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) predicted a normal monsoon season for the country. This forecast offers relief amid concerns about potential water scarcity.

articles.headline: Indian contingent prepares for Asian Games
articles.summary: Indian athletes are intensifying their training for the upcoming Asian Games. The contingent aims to improve upon its previous performance and secure more medals.

articles.headline: New study links air pollution to cognitive decline
articles.summary: A recent scientific study suggests a correlation between prolonged exposure to air pollution and accelerated cognitive decline in adults. Researchers recommend stricter pollution control measures.

articles.headline: Indian tech startups attract significant venture capital
articles.summary: Indian technology startups have successfully raised substantial venture capital funding in the recent quarter. This indicates growing investor confidence in the country's innovation ecosystem.

articles.headline: Government launches new digital literacy program
articles.summary: The government has rolled out a nationwide digital literacy program aimed at empowering citizens with essential online skills. The initiative targets rural and underserved communities.

articles.headline: Global leaders gather for climate change summit
articles.summary: World leaders convened for a summit focused on accelerating climate action. Discussions centered on renewable energy adoption and strategies to mitigate global warming.

articles.headline: Russia-Ukraine conflict continues to impact global energy markets
articles.summary: The ongoing conflict in Ukraine continues to disrupt global energy markets, leading to volatile oil and gas prices. International efforts to stabilize supply are underway.

articles.headline: WHO warns of rising antibiotic resistance
articles.summary: The World Health Organization issued a stark warning about the escalating threat of antibiotic resistance. The agency urged for responsible use of antibiotics and development of new treatments.

articles.headline: Indian film festival showcases diverse cinematic talent
articles.summary: An international film festival in Mumbai celebrated diverse cinematic works from around the globe. The event highlighted emerging filmmakers and international collaborations.

articles.headline: New high-speed rail project gets green light
articles.summary: A significant high-speed rail corridor project has received government approval. The initiative aims to improve inter-city connectivity and reduce travel times.

articles.headline: India boosts defense exports
articles.summary: India's defense exports have seen a notable increase, reaching new markets. The government aims to position India as a major defense manufacturing hub.

articles.headline: Scientists discover new exoplanet with potential for life
articles.summary: Astronomers have discovered a new exoplanet within a habitable zone, raising hopes for finding extraterrestrial life. Further observations are planned to study its atmosphere.

articles.headline: Country grapples with rising unemployment figures
articles.summary: Latest economic data reveals a slight increase in unemployment rates across several sectors. The government is exploring measures to boost job creation and economic recovery.

articles.headline: AI advancements transforming healthcare diagnostics
articles.summary: Artificial intelligence is making significant strides in healthcare, particularly in improving the accuracy and speed of medical diagnostics. This technology promises earlier disease detection.

articles.headline: Indian textile industry seeks government support
articles.summary: The Indian textile sector has appealed to the government for policy support to enhance competitiveness and overcome challenges in the global market.

articles.headline: Wildlife conservation efforts intensified in national parks
articles.summary: Conservationists are stepping up efforts to protect endangered species within national parks. New strategies are being implemented to combat poaching and habitat loss.

articles.headline: Supreme Court upholds freedom of press
articles.summary: The Supreme Court affirmed the fundamental right to freedom of the press, emphasizing its crucial role in a democratic society. The ruling addresses recent concerns about media restrictions.

articles.headline: Cricket legend retires from international matches
articles.summary: A celebrated Indian cricketer announced his retirement from all forms of international cricket, marking the end of an illustrious career. Tributes have poured in from fans and peers.

articles.headline: New COVID-19 variant detected, health officials monitoring
articles.summary: Health authorities are monitoring a newly identified COVID-19 variant. While early data is being analyzed, vigilance and adherence to safety protocols remain important.

articles.headline: Government revises agricultural policies
articles.summary: The government has announced revisions to agricultural policies aimed at boosting farmer incomes and modernizing farming practices. The changes focus on sustainable agriculture.

articles.headline: India pushes for greater international climate finance
articles.summary: India is advocating for increased international financial commitments to support climate action in developing nations. The country emphasizes shared responsibility in tackling climate change.

articles.headline: Robotics advancements in manufacturing sector
articles.summary: The manufacturing sector is witnessing significant integration of robotics to enhance efficiency and productivity. This technological shift is reshaping industrial operations.

articles.headline: Supreme Court stays controversial infrastructure project
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has issued a stay order on a controversial infrastructure development project, citing environmental concerns. The ruling is a victory for conservation advocates.

articles.headline: Indian diaspora plays key role in economic growth
articles.summary: A report highlights the significant contribution of the Indian diaspora to the country's economic growth through remittances and investment. Their role in global trade is also noted.

articles.headline: New electric vehicle policy unveiled
articles.summary: The government has launched a comprehensive policy to promote electric vehicles and charging infrastructure. The aim is to reduce carbon emissions and boost sustainable transportation.

articles.headline: Educational institutions adopt new teaching methodologies
articles.summary: Indian educational institutions are embracing innovative teaching methodologies to improve learning outcomes. Emphasis is placed on critical thinking and practical skills development.

articles.headline: Archaeologists uncover ancient city ruins
articles.summary: Archaeologists have unearthed significant ruins of an ancient city, providing new insights into India's historical past. The discovery is expected to draw international attention.

articles.headline: Space agency successfully launches communication satellite
articles.summary: India's space agency announced the successful launch of a new communication satellite. The satellite will enhance telecommunications and broadcasting services across the country.

articles.headline: Automobile sector faces supply chain disruptions
articles.summary: The Indian automobile sector is experiencing challenges due to ongoing global supply chain disruptions. Manufacturers are seeking solutions to mitigate the impact on production.

articles.headline: International trade fair focuses on renewable energy
articles.summary: A major international trade fair showcased advancements in renewable energy technologies. The event aimed to foster collaborations and investments in green energy solutions.

articles.headline: Yoga gains global recognition as wellness practice
articles.summary: Yoga continues to receive widespread global recognition as a holistic wellness practice. International interest in yoga retreats and teacher training programs is growing.

articles.headline: Road infrastructure development accelerates in rural areas
articles.summary: Significant progress is being made in developing road infrastructure in rural areas, improving connectivity and accessibility. This initiative aims to boost rural economies.

articles.headline: Government tightens regulations on social media platforms
articles.summary: The government has introduced new regulations for social media platforms, focusing on content moderation and user data protection. The move aims to curb misinformation.

articles.headline: India participates in global cybersecurity summit
articles.summary: India actively participated in a global cybersecurity summit, emphasizing collaboration to combat cyber threats. Discussions focused on enhancing digital security measures.

articles.headline: Olympic medalist announces retirement from professional sports
articles.summary: A prominent Indian Olympic medalist announced their retirement from professional sports, concluding a distinguished career. They expressed gratitude for the support received.

articles.headline: New strain of bird flu detected in poultry farms
articles.summary: Health authorities have confirmed the detection of a new strain of bird flu in poultry farms. Surveillance and containment measures are being implemented to prevent spread.

articles.headline: India strengthens ties with Southeast Asian nations
articles.summary: India is enhancing diplomatic and economic ties with several Southeast Asian nations. Discussions focused on regional cooperation and trade agreements.

articles.headline: Parliamentary committee reviews national education policy
articles.summary: A parliamentary committee is reviewing the implementation of the national education policy. The review aims to assess progress and identify areas for improvement.

articles.headline: World Bank predicts steady economic growth for India
articles.summary: The World Bank forecasts steady economic growth for India in the coming fiscal year. The report attributes this projection to domestic demand and policy reforms.

articles.headline: Indian Navy commissions new indigenous warship
articles.summary: The Indian Navy commissioned a new indigenous warship, enhancing its maritime defense capabilities. The vessel is equipped with advanced weaponry and technology.

articles.headline: Researchers develop new sustainable material
articles.summary: Scientists have developed a novel sustainable material with diverse applications, potentially reducing reliance on conventional resources. The material is biodegradable and eco-friendly.

articles.headline: PM Modi addresses global investors at economic forum
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi addressed global investors at an economic forum, highlighting investment opportunities in India. He emphasized the country's robust economic outlook.

articles.headline: How the ibis has learned to thrive in urban areas
articles.summary: Neha Sinha writes about the Red-naped ibis, a bird species thriving in Indian cities despite urbanization, unlike other ibis species globally. Its adaptability in diet and nesting sites, even in disturbed environments, showcases its resilience. The State of India’s Birds 2023 report notes an increase in its population.

articles.headline: FIFA introduces hydration breaks, disrupting World Cup game rhythm
articles.summary: FIFA's mandatory hydration breaks, half-time interviews, and advertising during the 2026 World Cup are criticized for disrupting game momentum and Americanizing football for commercial gain, aiming for an $11 billion windfall. This changes the sport's spirit, as observed in the Mexico vs. South Africa match, despite fan and player resistance.

articles.headline: Poorna Jagannathan shines as badass aunty in 'Deli Boys'
articles.summary: Raja Sen reviews 'Deli Boys' (Jio Hotstar), highlighting Poorna Jagannathan's portrayal of a formidable "aunty" leading a criminal empire. The series celebrates "brownness" with its cultural references and comedic take on drug dealing and money laundering. It's a six-episode show that doesn't take itself seriously but feels important due to Jagannathan's performance.

articles.headline: Post-DEI landscape reveals true LGBTQ+ inclusion beyond rainbow campaigns
articles.summary: Sandip Roy explores the post-DEI (Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion) landscape in India, highlighting efforts for genuine LGBTQ+ inclusion beyond superficial Pride month campaigns. He discusses a Kolkata mural depicting transgender lives through traditional crafts and a pop-up event for Varta's queer-trans education scholarships, emphasizing meaningful intersectionality.

articles.headline: Book review: 'The Wealth Networks' explores India's commercial history
articles.summary: Sundeep Khanna reviews Akshay Chavan’s 'The Wealth Networks', which integrates archaeology, mythology, and commerce to map India’s historical trade networks. The book showcases how regions like Sopara and Ajanta thrived as commercial crossroads, influenced by cotton routes and royal patronage, highlighting India's sophisticated commercial civilization.

articles.headline: Airport lounges transform into fine-dining destinations globally
articles.summary: Airport lounges, especially Business and First Class, are evolving beyond basic amenities into fine-dining experiences. Lounges like Singapore Airlines' SilverKris, Qantas' First Lounge, and Air France's La Première offer celebrity-chef curated menus, live cooking stations, and extensive wine lists, reflecting a shift in traveler expectations towards quality and personalization.

articles.headline: Arunachal's Gobuk village offers unique green tea experience
articles.summary: Aravinda Anantharaman recounts a visit to Gobuk village in Arunachal Pradesh, meeting 70-something-year-old headman Koni Miyu, who grows and makes exceptional green tea. The traditional bamboo house experience, with roasted shiitake mushrooms by the fire, highlights the village's biodiversity and Miyu's three decades of tea cultivation, now finding markets beyond local towns.

articles.headline: Filmmaker Shubhra Chatterji finds self-discovery in food research
articles.summary: Shubhra Chatterji reflects on her 15 years of documenting India's food habits, revealing a parallel journey of self-discovery. Her field notes, from recipe scribbles on pharmaceutical pads to a 2008 Granta notebook, trace her professional and personal evolution, culminating in a play about her grandparents' migration, intertwining work and life.

articles.headline: 50-plus Indian women redefine fashion on social media
articles.summary: Pooja Singh spotlights a growing cohort of Indian women in their 50s and 60s who challenge age-related fashion stereotypes on social media. Digital creators like Shonima Kaul, Adity Chakravarti, Mukta Singh, and Vinita Chaitanya celebrate individual style, advocate self-care, and offer fashion mentorship centered on timeless pieces and confidence, attracting significant audiences.

articles.headline: Barrel jeans and trousers emerge as 2026's defining fashion silhouette
articles.summary: Manish Mishra details the barrel silhouette, a key fashion trend for 2026, seen from designers to high-street brands. Defined by a mid-to-high-rise waist, widening hips/thighs, and tapering ankles, it offers comfort with a structured aesthetic. Experts advise on fabric choice and pairing with fitted or cropped tops to flatter various body types and occasions.

articles.headline: Barrackpore: Bengal's unlikely biryani capital built by Dada Boudi
articles.summary: Barrackpore has transformed into Bengal’s hottest biryani destination, largely due to Dada Boudi restaurant, known for generous portions and robust flavors. This phenomenon has attracted Kolkata's biggest brands, creating fierce competition. The article traces Dada Boudi’s origins from a simple pice hotel in the 1960s, shaped by migration and the evolving Bengali palate, making biryani a cultural staple.

articles.headline: Kenyan savanna safari teaches patience and presence
articles.summary: Meeti Shroff-Shah recounts her Kenyan safari experience, highlighting how observing wildlife in Masai Mara and Amboseli National Park required letting go of rigid time management and embracing the savanna's unhurried rhythm. Encounters with mating lions, feasting lion cubs, and a waiting cheetah taught her to be present and watchful, redefining her relationship with time and control.

articles.headline: Montenegro offers underrated luxury coastal escape in Boka Bay
articles.summary: Teja Lele describes Montenegro as a quiet Adriatic luxury destination, resisting the fast pace of its neighbors. Boka Bay's baroque towns like Perast, with its Venetian and Ottoman history, offer a blend of cultures reflected in its food and architecture. The country, with its stunning views and unhurried rhythm, provides room to breathe, distinct from crowded coasts.

articles.headline: YouTuber-directors like Kane Parsons and Curry Barker dominate horror box office
articles.summary: Aditya Mani Jha analyzes the success of YouTuber-directors like Kane Parsons ('Backrooms') and Curry Barker ('Obsession'), whose horror films, made on small budgets, have grossed over $250 million each. Their digital-native storytelling, blending awkward humor and horror, resonates with younger, genre-savvy audiences, mirroring online existence and benefiting from direct audience feedback, influencing mainstream cinema.

articles.headline: Sarita Handa built global textile empire on traditional crafts
articles.summary: Pooja Singh profiles Sarita Handa, founder of the eponymous luxury lifestyle label, who built a global-first business using traditional Indian textiles like patchwork quilts, crewel embroidery, and chikankari. Starting in 1992 with ₹10 lakh, her company now generates $82 million in revenue, primarily from exports to major retailers like Macy's and Pottery Barn, showcasing a journey shaped by passion, resilience, and learning.

articles.headline: Google builds rival AI chip business using Nvidia's strategy
articles.summary: Google is aggressively expanding its AI chip business (TPUs) by mirroring Nvidia's playbook, offering financial guarantees to data centers and striking deals with major AI labs like Anthropic. While Nvidia dominates the market, Google's massive financial power and technical development position it as a formidable challenger, aiming to monetize its hardware, previously kept in-house, and sell chips directly to customers.

articles.headline: Apple faces memory crunch, raises prices despite cash reserves
articles.summary: Apple is increasing product prices due to surging costs for DRAM memory chips, driven by booming AI demand. CEO Tim Cook admitted the situation is "unsustainable," with prices for high-end smartphone DRAM expected to surge 83%. This squeeze disadvantages Apple's business model, impacting gross margins and challenging its ability to power new AI features, despite its $62 billion cash reserves.

articles.headline: Jio Platforms outlines satellite, 5G, AI strategy for growth
articles.summary: Jio Platforms plans to drive its next growth phase through satellite communications and AI, aiming to build a sovereign low-earth orbit (LEO) satellite constellation for India and migrate all subscribers to 5G by 2030. The company will partner with global satellite operators and lease capacity to accelerate broadband rollout while also marketing its end-to-end 5G technology stack internationally.

articles.headline: Jio Platforms warns of Arpu risks, customer resistance to tariff hikes
articles.summary: Jio Platforms' DRHP warns that future tariff increases may not translate into higher revenue due to customer resistance, regulatory intervention, and intense competition. The company's monthly Average Revenue Per User (Arpu) is ₹214, trailing Bharti Airtel's ₹257. Jio also flagged its indebtedness, dependence on specific vendors, and social media bans for minors as potential business risks.

articles.headline: Jio targets $230 billion global market with 5G, FWA, and feature phone tech
articles.summary: Jio Platforms aims to expand its telecom technology globally, targeting a $230 billion market opportunity across 5G networks, fixed wireless access (FWA), and its feature phone operating system. The company plans to market its end-to-end 5G stack to international operators and sees a $15 billion opportunity from migrating 2G users worldwide, with 729 million customers still on 2G handsets.

articles.headline: Indian equities close week higher despite Friday's IT-led sell-off
articles.summary: Indian equities ended the week higher, with the Sensex gaining 1.69% and Nifty 1.65%, driven by financials, realty, and capital goods. Despite a nearly 1% drop on Friday due to profit-booking in IT stocks and AI concerns, renewed foreign inflows, softer crude prices, and the US-Iran peace deal supported market sentiment. However, a rainfall deficit poses a risk to rural demand.

articles.headline: Mukesh Ambani confirms generational transfer of Reliance leadership
articles.summary: Mukesh Ambani announced the near-complete transfer of Reliance Industries' day-to-day operations to his three children: Isha, Akash, and Anant, who will head consumer, technology, and energy businesses respectively. Ambani emphasized they share a "founder's mindset" and will work towards "one single indivisible Reliance," addressing concerns about potential business splits.

articles.headline: Government plans HR overhaul for state-run banks to boost transparency
articles.summary: The finance ministry is preparing a sweeping HR overhaul for state-run banks, aiming for more transparent transfers, promotions, and recruitment processes for their over 750,000 employees. Changes include Aadhaar-based verification for IBPS exams, digital portals for transfers, and staggered recruitment timelines for SBI, nationalized, and regional rural banks to reduce vacancies.

articles.headline: Amanda Lacaze cracked China’s grip on rare earths with Lynas
articles.summary: Amanda Lacaze, CEO of Australian mining company Lynas, successfully revitalized the firm by fixing its struggling rare-earth processing plant in Malaysia and securing a Pentagon deal. Her efforts challenged China's near-total dominance, ensuring alternative sources for strategically important heavy rare earths amid booming global demand for electronics, automobiles, and defense industries, despite initial industry skepticism.

articles.headline: Sebi to release new study on retail derivatives losses in July
articles.summary: The Securities and Exchange Board of India (Sebi) is preparing a new study on India’s derivatives market, focusing on mounting losses among retail investors. Sebi Chairman Tuhin Kanta Pandey announced that the study is underway and expected to be released in July.

articles.headline: FSSAI issues notices to food businesses for misleading claims
articles.summary: The Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI) has issued notices to several food businesses, including Bikanervala and Marico, for alleged misleading claims, labelling violations, and consumer complaints. FSSAI demands scientific substantiation for claims like "Good Fats Balance" and "Losorb for less oil absorption."

articles.headline: Cred approaches 10 million biometric UPI users for payments
articles.summary: Fintech startup Cred is close to reaching 10 million users utilizing its biometric authentication feature for UPI payments, according to an internal message from founder Kunal Shah. This milestone indicates the company's expansion in its payments services.

articles.headline: IMD predicts South-West monsoon advance into more states by Tuesday
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) announced favorable conditions for the South-West monsoon to advance further into Maharashtra, Telangana, Odisha, Jharkhand, Bihar, and Chhattisgarh by Tuesday. IMD projects seasonal rainfall at 90% of the long period average, indicating below-normal rains.

articles.headline: SBI Mutual Fund secures Sebi approval for IPO launch
articles.summary: SBI Mutual Fund, India’s largest fund house, has received approval from Sebi to launch its initial public offering (IPO). The proposed offering will be an offer for sale of up to 203.7 million equity shares.

articles.headline: RBI MPC holds repo rate amid West Asia conflict, inflation uncertainty
articles.summary: The RBI's Monetary Policy Committee voted to keep the repo rate unchanged at 5.25% at its last meeting, with Governor Sanjay Malhotra preferring a "wait and watch" approach. Concerns over the West Asia conflict, supply chain disruptions, and monsoon spread led to high uncertainty in inflation and growth projections, justifying holding rates.

articles.headline: Maxivision Super Specialty Eye Hospitals plans West India expansion ahead of IPO
articles.summary: G.S.K. Velu-backed Maxivision Super Specialty Eye Hospitals plans a major expansion in western India as it prepares for an IPO next year. Velu aims to take his three healthcare ventures – Neuberg Diagnostics, Maxivision, and Trivitron Healthcare – public over the next three years, focusing on scaling businesses to become category leaders before listing.

articles.headline: Foreign investors pull capital from Indian BFSI, oil & gas sectors
articles.summary: Global fund managers are withdrawing capital from Indian equities, particularly banking, financial services, insurance (BFSI), and oil & gas sectors. They are chasing opportunities in AI infrastructure and semiconductors in the US, Taiwan, South Korea, and tactical emerging markets like China, due to valuation concerns, policy changes, and oil price uncertainty in India.

articles.headline: Delhi High Court upholds government's temporary ban on Telegram
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court rejected Telegram's plea challenging the Centre's temporary ban on the messaging platform, affirming the government's action under Section 69A of the IT Act was justified. The ban was issued to curb alleged misuse by paper-leak and cheating networks ahead of the NEET-UG re-examination, with the court ruling that Section 69A allows blocking an entire platform.

articles.headline: Urban Harvest in talks to raise ₹300 crore, eyes expansion
articles.summary: Urban Harvest, a B2B food-tech startup supplying ingredients to restaurants and cloud kitchens, is in advanced talks to raise ₹300 crore in fresh funding. The round, expected to value the New Delhi-based startup at ₹1,500 crore, aims to expand its warehouse network and enter new markets like Delhi, Hyderabad, Mumbai, and Jaipur.

articles.headline: Sebi reinstates open market share buybacks, approves AIF, muni bond reforms
articles.summary: Sebi approved a clutch of reforms, reinstating open market share buybacks, accelerating alternative investment fund (AIF) launches, and enabling pooled borrowing by municipalities. Open market buybacks were barred a year ago due to tax and shareholder participation issues. The new framework allows faster AIF scheme launches and a 'Quick Transmission Processing' for small-value deceased investor claims.

articles.headline: Centre to deploy 100+ officers for VB-GRAM G rural employment transition
articles.summary: The Union government will deploy over 100 area officers to help states and Union territories transition to the Viksit Bharat – Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) (VB-GRAM G), its new rural employment framework launching 1 July. These officers will facilitate implementation, capacity-building, and address operational challenges during the transition from MGNREGA.

articles.headline: AI-driven exports shield Asia-Pacific economies from West Asia war shock
articles.summary: Asia-Pacific economies, including India, are weathering the West Asia war better than expected, thanks to surging AI-driven exports and investment. Moody's Analytics reports growth easing slightly to 4.1% in 2026 from 4.3% in 2025, as the AI boom's initial boost fades. While resilient, the region remains vulnerable to high oil prices, food inflation, trade tensions, and weather disruptions.

articles.headline: Highway monetization kitty may swell by ₹20,000 crore in FY27
articles.summary: The road transport and highways ministry considers raising its FY27 highway monetization target by ₹15,000-20,000 crore, potentially exceeding ₹50,000 crore. This shift includes private investment from build-operate-transfer (BOT) projects, aligning with NMP 2.0. The move aims to increase private capital in highway development and reduce reliance on budgetary support.

articles.headline: Tata Consumer plans distribution rejig for margin growth
articles.summary: Tata Consumer Products Ltd aims to expand margins by 70-100 basis points annually through a distribution rejig and strategic pricing. The company, which posted a 15% revenue increase in FY26, is strengthening its channel strategy, onboarding new distributors, and reworking its general trade segment to drive depth of distribution and increase retailing, focusing on core and growth businesses.

articles.headline: Domestic flows help cash market defy correction
articles.summary: Indian equities fell sharply in early 2026, but cash market turnover increased as mutual funds absorbed ₹2.44-lakh crore in FPI outflows (₹2.25-lakh crore), showing resilience unlike previous corrections. Domestic institutional investor (DII) ownership in Nifty 500 rose to 20.9% by March 2026. Higher minimum contract sizes affected small traders.

articles.headline: Finolex Cables: Buy for long-term gains
articles.summary: Finolex Cables (₹1,164) is bullish long-term, expected to reach ₹1,400-1,500 in months and ₹2,000 in a couple of years. Investors can buy now and accumulate on dips at ₹1,040 with a stop-loss at ₹820, trailing it as the price rises.

articles.headline: PM Modi inaugurates projects, hails Bengal's development under BJP
articles.summary: PM Narendra Modi announced multiple development projects for West Bengal spanning railways, agriculture, and fisheries on “Paschimbanga Divas”. He released the 23rd instalment of PM-Kisan (₹18,880 crore) and stated the new BJP government is acting against corruption and driving progress, marking an end to the “cut money” era.

articles.headline: Vivek Aggarwal becomes first Indian VP of FATF
articles.summary: Culture Secretary Vivek Aggarwal has been elected Vice-President of the Financial Action Task Force (FATF) for 2026-27, a first for India. This appointment recognizes India's strong anti-money laundering and counter-terrorist financing framework and its sustained contributions to shaping global policy on emerging risks.

articles.headline: Rupee recovery faces strong dollar and high US yields
articles.summary: The Indian rupee recovered to 94.33 after hitting a low of 96.96 in May, aided by falling crude oil prices and RBI's FCNR(B) swap window. However, a strong dollar and rising US Treasury yields could lead to weakening towards 96-97 and fresh lows by 2027. Technical analysis suggests a short-term range of 94-96, with a possible strengthening to 93-92.50 before weakening again.

articles.headline: Gold's safe haven status questioned amid price volatility
articles.summary: Gold has failed its safe-haven role recently, falling 16% in India (22% globally) since February, mainly due to rising US treasury yields, central bank sales, and ebbing ETF flows. While historically an effective hedge, its erratic behavior suggests constant portfolio allocation (10-15%) is key, rather than reacting to crises for returns.

articles.headline: Raptee.HV partners IDFC FIRST Bank for e-motorcycle financing
articles.summary: Chennai-based electric motorcycle OEM Raptee.HV partnered with IDFC FIRST Bank to offer attractive loan terms, faster approvals, and flexible repayment options. This aims to democratize electric motorcycle ownership in India, building on existing partnerships with Indian Overseas Bank (IOB) and Shriram Finance.

articles.headline: Big Tech's AI spending impacts share buybacks
articles.summary: The high cost of the AI race is reducing share buybacks by Big Tech giants like Alphabet, Microsoft, Meta, and Amazon. Only Microsoft bought back shares in Q1, its lowest in a decade. Some firms, like Alphabet (planning $85B equity sale) and Meta, are issuing new stock to fund AI ambitions, raising questions about returns on invested capital.

articles.headline: Zerodha Fund House launches India's first Life Cycle funds
articles.summary: Zerodha Fund House introduced India's first target-date maturity Life Cycle Fund series (2036, 2041), investing across equity, debt, and commodities with an asset allocation that shifts from growth to conservative as the target year approaches.

articles.headline: HDFC AMC revises creation unit sizes for select ETFs
articles.summary: HDFC Asset Management Company reduced creation unit sizes for direct transactions in select HDFC Mutual Fund ETF schemes, effective June 22. This includes HDFC NIFTY Private Bank ETF, HDFC NIFTY Bank ETF, HDFC NIFTY PSU Bank ETF, HDFC NIFTY50 Value 20 ETF, HDFC NIFTY100 Low Volatility 30 ETF, and HDFC NIFTY200 Momentum 30 ETF.

articles.headline: Nippon India Multi Cap Fund shows strong performance
articles.summary: Nippon India Multi Cap Fund (NMCF), with ₹53,411 crore AUM, has delivered the highest five-year return among multi-cap funds and a 17.4% CAGR since 2005. It maintains a disciplined 40-45% large-cap, 25-30% mid-cap, and 25-30% small-cap allocation, focusing on business quality, management, and valuation, with a low portfolio turnover.

articles.headline: Domestic equity schemes offer hidden global exposure
articles.summary: With overseas investment limits exhausted for dedicated international funds, 40 domestic equity schemes (totaling ₹35,941 crore) now offer indirect global exposure (up to 29% of portfolios) through flexi-cap, value, contra, multi-asset, hybrid, and sectoral funds. This provides geographical diversification and access to global leaders in niche sectors like AI and semiconductors.

articles.headline: Shyam Metalics poised for growth from value-added products
articles.summary: Shyam Metalics, trading at a premium valuation, is recommended for accumulation due to its expansion into value-added products like CR mills and aluminium foils, which are expected to drive top-line growth and margin improvement. The company plans ₹3,000 crore capex annually for three years, funded by internal accruals, with a net debt-to-EBITDA ratio of 0.2x.

articles.headline: Nifty, Nifty Bank gain as bearish sentiment eases
articles.summary: Nifty 50 and Nifty Bank gained 1.7% and 1.5% respectively last week, extending recovery driven by short covering. FIIs maintained a cautious stance despite reduced bearish futures exposure. Nifty futures (24,057) trade above key support, eyeing 24,380-24,550. Nifty Bank futures (57,862) show positive bias, targeting 59,000, with support at 56,600.

articles.headline: Understanding Open Interest in Nifty Index movements
articles.summary: Change in Open Interest (OI) can indicate near-term Nifty Index movements. A significant OI increase in a call strike suggests shorts believe the index won't surpass that level, while for a put strike, shorts expect the index to stay above. This metric is best used as additional evidence for the Nifty Index, not individual stocks or futures, given its higher risk for shorts.

articles.headline: Gold and silver futures face further decline
articles.summary: Gold ($4,155/ounce, ₹1,47,203/10 gm) and silver ($64.9/ounce, ₹2,33,185/kg) depreciated last week by 1.5-2.2% and 4.6-5.3% respectively. Both are expected to decline further, with gold potentially reaching ₹1,41,000 and silver ₹2,20,000, unless strong resistance levels (gold ₹1,50,000, silver ₹2,55,000) are breached.

articles.headline: Crude oil trend remains bearish despite potential short-term rise
articles.summary: Brent crude oil futures ($80.60/barrel) and domestic crude oil futures (₹7,262/barrel) slipped 7.7-8.9% last week, breaking important support. The outlook remains bearish, though a corrective rise to $86-92 (₹8,000-8,200) is possible before a potential further fall to $73 (₹6,000). Traders are advised to stay out for now and short on a rise to ₹7,900.

articles.headline: Benchmark indices show positive bias, eye new highs
articles.summary: Nifty, Sensex, and Nifty Bank rallied over 1.5% last week, driven by the US-Iran peace deal. Nifty and Sensex are expected to breach immediate resistance (24,250 and 77,800) and rise further. Nifty Bank (57,685.75) may oscillate before a bullish breakout. Midcap and Smallcap indices also show positive momentum. FPIs ended their selling spree, buying $251 million in equities.

articles.headline: Ashok Leyland stock outlook: Accumulate on dips
articles.summary: Ashok Leyland (₹157) shows a bullish bias from its 21-Month Moving Average support. Expect a rise past ₹173 to target ₹270 in 1-2 years. Accumulate at ₹149, keep stop-loss at ₹128. Move stop-loss to ₹163 at ₹180, and higher to ₹195, ₹220 at ₹215, ₹245 respectively. Exit at ₹260.

articles.headline: Persistent Systems: Accumulate in tranches for long term
articles.summary: Persistent Systems (₹4,728) is weak short-term, vulnerable to fall to ₹4,200-4,000. For 3-4 years, buy in three tranches at ₹4,230, ₹4,110, and ₹3,760. Keep stop-loss at ₹3,120. Revise stop-loss higher to ₹4,640, ₹5,380, and ₹5,850 as price rises to ₹5,120, ₹5,750, and ₹6,050 respectively. Exit at ₹6,380.

articles.headline: Jupiter Life Line Hospitals: Stay out for now
articles.summary: Jupiter Life Line Hospitals (₹1,336) is in a downtrend. Resistance is at ₹1,430-1,450. A downward reversal from this zone could drag the price to ₹1,200-1,000. A strong break above ₹1,450 is needed to end the downtrend and signal a rise to ₹1,800-1,900. It is better to stay out of this stock for now.

articles.headline: Inox Wind: Exit holdings due to weak trend
articles.summary: Inox Wind (₹90) has a long-term downtrend with strong resistances at ₹95 and ₹110. It is vulnerable to fall to ₹63 or even ₹43. A strong break above ₹110 is needed for a relief rally to ₹140, but only past ₹150 would signal the end of the downtrend. Advised to exit the stock and accept the loss.

articles.headline: Fed Chairman Warsh shifts to stripped-down central banking
articles.summary: US Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh has adopted a "stripped-down, 1990s-style" central banking approach, emphasizing inflation and reducing detailed policy commentary. This move, aiming for clarity, has sparked market reactions, with investors anticipating a rate hike soon, and raises questions about its sustainability in a complex world.

articles.headline: Aurobindo Pharma regains momentum, targets ₹1,700
articles.summary: Aurobindo Pharma (₹1,493.60) has resumed its uptrend since February, rebounding after a moderation from ₹1,550. The stock is expected to advance further to ₹1,700 in the medium term. Traders can buy now and accumulate at ₹1,425, with a stop-loss at ₹1,370. Adjust stop-loss as price rises, and liquidate at ₹1,700.

articles.headline: Bharat Electronics rebounds from support, eyes ₹500
articles.summary: Bharat Electronics (BEL) (₹427.15) rallied 5.1% last week, rebounding for the third time from ₹400 support this year, indicating strong buying interest. The stock is likely to retest the ₹460-470 resistance band, and a breakout could lift it to ₹500. Traders can buy at ₹427 and ₹415, with a stop-loss at ₹390. Exit balance at ₹500.

articles.headline: Trent confirms bullish reversal, target ₹4,000
articles.summary: Trent (₹3,204.25) reversed its two-year downtrend after hitting a low in April, confirming an inverted head and shoulder pattern and breaking out of a key barrier at ₹2,970. The stock is expected to extend its rally to ₹4,000 in the coming months. Participants can go long now and buy more shares if the price dips to ₹2,970. Stop-loss at ₹2,600.

articles.headline: RBI tightens norms against mis-selling of financial products
articles.summary: RBI's new directions, effective January 1, 2027, crack down on mis-selling of financial products by regulated entities. Key changes include mandatory explicit consent for sales, banning forced bundling, prohibiting deceptive "dark patterns" in digital interfaces, and increased accountability for sales agents. Entities must also refund and compensate for mis-selling.

articles.headline: Health insurance sum insured increase often doesn't require new medical tests
articles.summary: Increasing health insurance sum insured, especially for a 30-year-old in good health with a reasonable coverage hike, often doesn't require fresh medical tests. Insurers use risk-based assessments considering age, pre-existing conditions, and increase extent. IRDAI reforms simplify processes, but full disclosure of health changes remains crucial. Reassess sum insured every 2-3 years.

articles.headline: Data quality scores for SFBs, private banks dip in March
articles.summary: The Supervisory Data Quality Index (SDQI) scores for small finance banks (SFBs) and private sector banks (PVBs) declined by 1.5 and 1.3 points respectively in March compared to December 2025. Public sector banks also saw a marginal dip (0.3), while foreign banks improved their score by 0.7 points to 91.4. The overall SDQI for scheduled commercial banks declined by 0.2 points.

articles.headline: India's LPG consumption projected to double by 2050
articles.summary: India's LPG consumption is projected to double to 2 million barrels per day (mb/d) by 2050, driven primarily by the residential sector (90% of demand) and an expanding petrochemicals segment. Cumulative LPG consumption rose 6% y-o-y to 33.21 mt in FY26. India is expected to contribute 8.1 mb/d to global oil demand growth by 2050.

articles.headline: DGCA to integrate AI and ML for aviation regulation 2.0
articles.summary: India's civil aviation regulator is upgrading its eGCA platform to eGCA 2.0, incorporating AI, machine learning (ML), blockchain, and predictive surveillance. This digital transformation aims to enhance regulatory oversight and streamline approvals, with a significant weightage on emerging technologies and cybersecurity expertise in its technology partner selection. The initiative lays foundation for a technology-enabled regulatory ecosystem.

articles.headline: Andhra Pradesh partners Canva to provide AI tool access for education
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh government has partnered with Canva to provide its AI-powered creative design tool to all government school students, teachers, and Education Department officials. Canva will integrate with the state's LEAP App, Google Workspace, and Chromebooks, offering phased capacity-building support and promoting responsible AI use in classrooms.

articles.headline: Zepto slashes customer acquisition costs ahead of IPO
articles.summary: Quick commerce firm Zepto significantly improved its unit economics ahead of its IPO, primarily by reducing digital marketing costs per order from ₹33.75 (FY25) to ₹1.01 (March 2026). This efficiency boost, stemming from increased repeat customers and in-house tools, outpaces modest savings from supply chain operations (₹1.79 per order).

articles.headline: West Asia crisis drives container freight rates soaring
articles.summary: The West Asia conflict has caused container freight spot rates to soar, with Far East-US West Coast rates up 192% since February 28. Experts predict rates will continue climbing until the Strait of Hormuz fully reopens, potentially easing gradually post-September, but remaining above pre-crisis levels due to re-balancing networks and elevated insurance costs.

articles.headline: Karnataka cabinet clears new dept to address public grievances
articles.summary: The Karnataka cabinet approved the 'Prajaseva Department' to receive and address public grievances directly. District in-charge ministers will hold weekly public meetings in assembly segments.

articles.headline: Bengaluru schools ban food delivery apps on campus
articles.summary: Several Bengaluru schools have banned food delivery apps on campus, citing health, hygiene, safety, and logistical concerns. They also aim to reduce disparity among students.

articles.headline: NEET retake today; 22.8 lakh aspirants to appear
articles.summary: Over 22.8 lakh students will take the NEET-UG re-examination today across India and 14 centres abroad. The National Testing Agency has implemented comprehensive security measures.

articles.headline: 16 fixed-dose combo medicines banned over safety, efficacy concerns
articles.summary: The government has banned 16 fixed-dose combination medicines, including antibiotics and painkillers, after expert panels found them scientifically unjustified and potentially risky.

articles.headline: Witkoff, Kushner in Switzerland; Talks today, but Tehran shuts Hormuz
articles.summary: US negotiators are in Switzerland for talks with Iran on their interim nuclear agreement. Iran announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, citing Israeli attacks in Lebanon.

articles.headline: Bank manager cheats man of 2.6cr, held
articles.summary: An Axis Bank manager and two associates were arrested for duping a businessman of Rs 2.6 crore by posing as logistics and construction company owners.

articles.headline: FSSAI officials intercept over 30 vehicles carrying cheap milk
articles.summary: FSSAI officials intercepted over 30 vehicles carrying unregulated milk from Tamil Nadu into Bengaluru, citing lack of traceability and hygiene concerns, sold at significantly lower prices.

articles.headline: GBA cracks down on lake pollution
articles.summary: Greater Bengaluru Authority chief has directed officials to improve water quality in 32 polluted lakes identified as 'Class E' by KSPCB, with action against sewage discharge and encroachments.

articles.headline: CET option entry begins; mock seat allotment result on July 6
articles.summary: Karnataka Examinations Authority has opened option entry for CET admissions, dropping pre-round counselling. Mock seat allotment results will be out on July 6, final on July 15.

articles.headline: 48 hours on, one body recovered from STP; other missing
articles.summary: One of two workers who fell into a BWSSB sewage treatment plant in Balagere has been recovered after two days. The second worker remains missing amidst challenging rescue operations.

articles.headline: Manual scavenging continues despite ban; 7,483 identified
articles.summary: Fifty-two years after Karnataka banned manual scavenging, the practice persists with 7,483 individuals identified in 24 districts. The work remains rooted in caste, poverty, and lack of rehabilitation.

articles.headline: 2k to take part in Yoga Day event at Vidhana Soudha
articles.summary: Over 2,000 yoga practitioners will gather at Vidhana Soudha for International Yoga Day. The event aligns with the theme 'Yoga for Healthy Ageing' and will feature mass demonstrations.

articles.headline: Bowring wall collapse work stuck in red tape; KRIDL steps in
articles.summary: Reconstruction of the Bowring Hospital compound wall, which collapsed two months ago killing seven, is stalled due to administrative hurdles. KRIDL has been tasked with the work.

articles.headline: College girl assaulted by drunk men after phone row
articles.summary: A BBA student was assaulted by youths at a party in Bengaluru after a dispute over a mobile phone. Police have registered a case against six individuals.

articles.headline: Overseas Kannadigas seek dedicated NRI secretariat
articles.summary: Overseas Kannadigas have urged the Karnataka government to establish a dedicated secretariat to address NRI issues, facilitate investments, and provide grievance redressal mechanisms.

articles.headline: Cabinet gives 15-day extension for bldgs seeking power sans OC
articles.summary: The Karnataka cabinet approved a 15-day extension for building owners to get permanent electricity connections without an Occupancy Certificate (OC).

articles.headline: Rope in UPSC for interview process: Min
articles.summary: Karnataka Minister Eshwar Khandre asked officials to entrust UPSC with the interview process for KRIDL recruitments, while the exam will be conducted by KEA.

articles.headline: Move may help DKS shake off anti-incumbency
articles.summary: The new grievance redressal department is seen as part of CM Shivakumar's effort to build a people-centric image and shake off anti-incumbency ahead of elections.

articles.headline: Will meet BJP president over cross-voting: HDK
articles.summary: JD(S) leader H D Kumaraswamy stated he will meet the BJP president regarding cross-voting in Legislative Council elections, alleging attempts to influence legislators.

articles.headline: Leopard scare: School closed, 28 kids stay home
articles.summary: A government school near the Bhadra Tiger Reserve has closed due to leopard attacks, keeping 28 children home for 10 days, sparking criticism of forest and revenue officials.

articles.headline: She went for her mother’s death cert, got her own
articles.summary: Shiggaon taluk administration mistakenly declared a living woman dead, leading to cancellation of her Aadhaar and ration cards. The error is being rectified.

articles.headline: NTA says Abu Dhabi choice made via aspirant’s login, family contests
articles.summary: NTA claims a NEET aspirant chose Abu Dhabi via their login, but the family contests this, stating their application listed Nagpur, Bhandara, and Wardha as preferred cities.

articles.headline: 22.8 lakh to take NEET (UG) re-test today across India
articles.summary: A total of 22.8 lakh students will take the NEET-UG re-test today across India. Over 18 lakh will use English papers, and 3.5 lakh will use Hindi papers.

articles.headline: Cyber Crime Police arrest three in Rukmini Vasanth deepfake case
articles.summary: Three people were arrested by Cyber Crime Police for creating a deepfake image of actor Rukmini Vasanth. The AI-generated image caused a stir online.

articles.headline: James Burrows, sitcom director, passes away at 85
articles.summary: Legendary director James Burrows, known for directing iconic sitcoms like Friends and Cheers, has passed away at 85. He won 11 Emmy Awards.

articles.headline: Managing your hours or innovative ideas
articles.summary: Aries are advised to focus on gaining knowledge for future success and mastering goals. It's a good week for long-pending contracts. Avoid heated exchanges.

articles.headline: The future of joint replacement lies in precision, not technology
articles.summary: Dr Dharmapal G K highlights that future joint replacement focuses on restoring natural anatomy and function through customized approaches and advanced technology like pin-less robotics.

articles.headline: Inside luxe retail’s music strategy
articles.summary: Luxury brands are using music to shape customer experience, evoke aspiration, and build emotional connections, turning stores into destinations.

articles.headline: Salt-and-pepper diamonds are making flaws fashionable
articles.summary: Salt-and-pepper diamonds, with natural inclusions, are gaining popularity for their uniqueness and sustainability, challenging traditional notions of diamond perfection.

articles.headline: Pankaj Tripathi’s heartwarming performance with his daughter
articles.summary: Pankaj Tripathi and his daughter Aashi star in a De Beers Group film, 'Love, From Dad,' celebrating their evolving relationship and the significance of natural diamonds.

articles.headline: 'Balaramana Dinagalu isn’t just another macho gangster film’
articles.summary: Actor Priya Anand believes 'Balaramana Dinagalu' offers emotional depth and a fresh take on gangster films, exploring the vulnerable side of characters.

articles.headline: When the teacher said, Call Your Father, Maine Kaha Done!
articles.summary: Filmmaker Guneet Monga shares anecdotes about her father's supportive and unconventional parenting style, which fostered her confidence and independence.

articles.headline: Modi announces 1,492cr devpt projects for Bengal
articles.summary: PM Modi announced projects worth over Rs 1,492 crore for West Bengal, focusing on agriculture, railways, connectivity, and healthcare, aiming to boost rural economy.

articles.headline: CJP vows to continue protest till Pradhan quits
articles.summary: Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) continued its protest at Jantar Mantar, demanding Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over the NEET-UG paper leak controversy.

articles.headline: Wider effort in Lahore to revisit pre-Partition past
articles.summary: Lahore is actively revisiting its pre-Partition history by restoring historic names and memorialising figures from undivided Punjab, reflecting a trend of cultural heritage revival.

articles.headline: Trump’s adulation often signals ‘ambush’: Experts
articles.summary: Experts suggest Trump's praise for leaders like Modi and Xi Jinping often precedes transactional demands, trade wars, or shifts in diplomatic alignments.

articles.headline: Delhi rly stn assault: 32-yr-old beaten to death
articles.summary: A 32-year-old Delhi Metro security guard died after being assaulted by passengers at Shahdara railway station. Eight individuals seen in a viral video have been detained.

articles.headline: Russia hits Zaporizhzhia with glide bombs, kills 4
articles.summary: Russian forces struck Zaporizhzhia with glide bombs, killing four people and injuring six. Residents may be trapped under damaged buildings.

articles.headline: Zelensky sends back his Polish honour after WWII row
articles.summary: Ukrainian President Zelensky returned a state decoration to Poland's President after the latter revoked it due to a dispute over honouring WWII-era Ukrainian insurgents.

articles.headline: US Silence Doubters To Storm Into KOs
articles.summary: USA secured first place in Group D with a 2-0 win over Australia, silencing doubters of coach Pochettino and the team's capabilities. Pepi's performance was key.

articles.headline: As Spain regroup, it’s all Yamal
articles.summary: Spain, unbeaten in 32 games, seeks a turnaround after a draw against Cabo Verde. Youngster Lamine Yamal is expected to be crucial for their future matches.

articles.headline: India Women finish whitewash Away Afghanistan
articles.summary: India secured a 3-0 whitewash against Afghanistan with Prasidh Krishna's five-wicket haul and Yashasvi Jaiswal's century, showcasing strong form ahead of the England series.

articles.headline: Brazil settle nerves against Haiti but bigger tests loom
articles.summary: Brazil secured their first World Cup win, defeating Haiti 3-0. Matheus Cunha and Vinicius Jr scored. The team faces Scotland next, with Raphinha potentially out.

articles.headline: Players, coaches had to pay for their flights
articles.summary: Curacao coach Dick Advocaat noted significant changes for the island nation's team, including players paying for flights and learning the anthem, benefiting the country's global recognition.

articles.headline: ‘Everybody’s crying’: Turkiye rack up 32 goal attempts
articles.summary: Turkiye scored zero goals despite 32 attempts against Paraguay and Turkiye, crashing out of the World Cup. Coach Arda Guler apologised to the nation.

articles.headline: ‘My dad & I were buddies, no hierarchy’
articles.summary: Filmmaker Guneet Monga recounts her father's belief in abundance and freedom, sharing how he supported her through car crashes and academic struggles.

articles.headline: ‘Meri beti jayegi America!’ – Father sold kada to fund her trip
articles.summary: Guneet Monga shares an emotional story of her father selling his gold kada to fund her trip to America, fostering her independence and belief in herself.

articles.headline: ‘Darna nahi hai life mein’
articles.summary: Pankaj Tripathi's philosophy of not fearing challenges, as shared by his daughter Aashi, reflects a parenting approach that builds resilience and confidence.

articles.headline: Understanding ovarian torsion: A hidden danger for teenage girls
articles.summary: Dr Manjula S Patil explains ovarian torsion, a critical emergency in teenage girls, where the ovary twists cutting off blood supply, stressing the need for prompt medical attention.

articles.headline: Building India’s leading biryani empire on trust and taste
articles.summary: Nandhana Palace, a prominent Andhra restaurant chain, attributes its success to authentic flavors, quality ingredients, and a family legacy of hospitality.

articles.headline: Baby, I’m yours x: Anne Hathaway announces pregnancy
articles.summary: Actress Anne Hathaway announced her third pregnancy with husband Adam Shulman via an Instagram video, sharing a heartwarming moment.

articles.headline: Hyderabad court summons Allu Arjun in stampede case
articles.summary: Actor Allu Arjun has been summoned by a Hyderabad court in connection with a stampede case during a film screening. Police have filed charges against 23 individuals.

articles.headline: NEET Retest: All hands on deck for unprecedented security
articles.summary: NEET-UG 2026 candidates retest today after May 3 exam scrapped due to paper leak. Over 2.27 million registered candidates will appear. Unprecedented security includes 200,000 personnel, IAF logistics for papers, and nationwide Telegram/message-editing disabled till June 22/30 respectively. Exam time increased by 15 minutes.

articles.headline: Man beaten to death by fellow passengers at Delhi station
articles.summary: A 32-year-old man was beaten to death by fellow passengers at Shahdara Railway Station after an argument over train boarding. Attackers were apprehended at Muzaffarnagar Railway Station and brought to Delhi for questioning.

articles.headline: Woman killed over parking dispute in Delhi
articles.summary: A 32-year-old woman was stabbed to death and her husband injured in Bindapur, southwest Delhi, in a row over parking with their neighbours. Police detained one accused, Pappu, and are tracing a minor involved.

articles.headline: PM Modi: Bengal finally on path envisioned in 1947
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi stated West Bengal is finally moving along a development path aligned with the cultural identity envisioned in 1947. Addressing 'Paschimbanga Divas' in Hooghly, Modi said attempts were made during Partition to include the entire state in Pakistan.

articles.headline: Bangladesh PM Rahman chooses Malaysia, China for first foreign visits
articles.summary: Bangladesh Prime Minister Tarique Rahman chose Malaysia and China for his first foreign visits to expand overseas employment opportunities and drum up investments, bypassing India. He will participate in the World Economic Forum in China and meet President Xi Jinping.

articles.headline: DTC to start bus service from 4am on select routes
articles.summary: The Delhi Transport Corporation (DTC) is likely to start additional bus services at 4am on select routes, following instructions from Lieutenant Governor Taranjit Singh Sandhu. The initiative aims to increase bus availability during pre-dawn hours for early commuters.

articles.headline: Telangana to charge all government bills on one portal
articles.summary: The Telangana government will roll out CURE-1, a common unified billing and collection system. This centralized digital platform will enable citizens to make multiple government payments via a single mobile application and web portal, initially in Hyderabad.

articles.headline: Former AIADMK minister and 500 others join TVK
articles.summary: Former Tamil Nadu minister Gomathi Srinivasan, veteran film director RB Udhayakumar, and 500 others, including AIADMK chief Edappadi K Palaniswami’s niece Maneka Balakrishnan, joined the Tamilaga Vetri Kazhagam (TVK). TVK leaders noted AIADMK cadres drifting towards them.

articles.headline: DMK, MNM urge TN speaker to broadcast assembly live
articles.summary: Opposition Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK) and Makkal Needhi Maiam (MNM) urged Tamil Nadu Assembly Speaker to ensure live telecast of the ongoing legislative session proceedings. They stated CM C Joseph Vijay's promise was not kept, with only one debate telecast live.

articles.headline: SC junks plea challenging ED raids in TASMAC PMLA probe
