articles.summary: The Supreme Court dismissed a businessman’s plea challenging the legality of search and seizure proceedings conducted by the Enforcement Directorate (ED) at his residence. The probe is linked to alleged money laundering in the Tamil Nadu State Marketing Corporation (TASMAC).

articles.headline: 2 held for sexual assault of students in Pocso case in Idukki
articles.summary: Two people, including a minor, were held on Saturday for allegedly sexually assaulting two school students at Munnar, Idukki. The adult accused was identified as Sharavanan from Didigul, and a minor was detained in the case.

articles.headline: Interim bail granted to minister's son in POCSO case
articles.summary: A city court granted interim bail to Bageerath, son of Union minister Bandi Sanjay Kumar, until June 25. He was arrested in connection with a POCSO case, and the bail allows him to write his BBA exams.

articles.headline: Over 140,000 aspirants to sit for NEET retest in Tamil Nadu
articles.summary: Over 140,000 aspirants from Tamil Nadu are expected to appear for the NEET re-examination on Sunday. The test will be conducted in more than 307 centres across 31 districts, with tight security arrangements in place.

articles.headline: Andhra CM stresses balance between tech and spirituality
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister N Chandrababu Naidu called for a balance between adopting technology and spirituality, including yoga, for a stress-free life. He attended a yoga session with Baba Ramdev and urged the present generation to adopt yoga as a way of life.

articles.headline: Nair Service Society denies second term to Kerala ex-minister
articles.summary: Former minister and actor K B Ganesh Kumar will no longer be a member of the Nair Service Society (NSS) board of directors as the organization decided against reappointing him. NSS denied allegations that the decision was undemocratic.

articles.headline: Karnataka minister: Not seeking more than Cauvery share
articles.summary: Karnataka Water Resources Minister Ramalinga Reddy stated the state seeks only its allocated share of Cauvery water and not 'even a single drop' more from Tamil Nadu. He reiterated that the proposed Mekedatu project would benefit farmers in both states.

articles.headline: Mock drill, cyber surveillance ahead of NEET retest
articles.summary: States nationwide stepped up security and surveillance for the NEET-UG re-examination, with authorities conducting drills, deploying cyber teams, and arranging special transport. Measures include CCTV, jammers, and biometric verification to ensure transparent conduct.

articles.headline: Rahul Gandhi urges govt to ensure no glitches in NEET retest
articles.summary: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi urged the government to ensure there are no 'glitches' or 'irregularities' in the NEET-UG re-examination. He stated that students have been 'through enough stress and they really cannot afford to go through it again.'

articles.headline: Shah: Only one Shiv Sena, led by Eknath Shinde
articles.summary: Union home minister Amit Shah declared there is only one Shiv Sena, headed by Maharashtra deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde, at a Kolhapur rally. He added that illegal migrants from neighbouring countries would be identified and deported from India.

articles.headline: PM Modi wishes President Murmu on 68th birthday
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi planted a sapling with President Droupadi Murmu on her 68th birthday, at Pahadpur village in Odisha’s Mayurbhanj district. The event coincided with the second anniversary celebrations of the state’s BJP government.

articles.headline: 14.3 million UP students to join Yoga Day celebrations
articles.summary: Around 14.3 million students in over 132,000 government schools and 746 Kasturba Gandhi Balika Vidyalayas across Uttar Pradesh will participate in mass Yoga sessions on International Yoga Day, June 21. Collective Yoga sessions will be held in schools statewide.

articles.headline: Buses arranged for NEET aspirants amid BEST strike
articles.summary: Commuters faced inconvenience for the second day due to the ongoing BEST strike in Mumbai. To help students taking the NEET retest on Sunday, the Brihanmumbai Electric Supply and Transport (BEST) has arranged 60 additional buses across the city.

articles.headline: Murmu: India always spreads peace in the world
articles.summary: President Droupadi Murmu stated India consistently advocates peace and dialogue during global conflicts, stressing that disputes can be resolved only through mutual discussions, not war. She highlighted India's humanitarian role during global crises and PM Modi's leadership.

articles.headline: President to confer Padma Awards on June 23
articles.summary: President Droupadi Murmu will confer 65 Padma Awards, including two Padma Vibhushan, seven Padma Bhushan, and 56 Padma Shri, at the second civil investiture ceremony on Tuesday. Recipients include tennis legend Vijay Amritraj, actor Mammootty, playback singer Alka Yagnik, and cricketer Rohit Sharma.

articles.headline: BJP blames rival parties' splits on lack of leadership
articles.summary: The BJP rejected allegations of engineering rebellion in opposition parties like TMC and Shiv Sena (UBT), blaming recent upheavals on 'leadership deficit.' The ruling party believes it stands to benefit as rebel groups show inclination to support the NDA.

articles.headline: HC orders Centre to take down fake posts on judges
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court directed the Union government to take down viral posts falsely claiming Chief Justice of India Surya Kant and other judges attended a badminton tournament in UK at public expense. The court observed such false information brought disrepute to judicial institutions.

articles.headline: 3 cops shunted for sodomising cancer patient in Haryana
articles.summary: Three police officers were suspended for allegedly sexually assaulting a bone cancer patient inside a police station in Kurukshetra district, Haryana. An FIR has been registered against ASIs Sanjeev Kumar and Rajendra Kumar, and Home Guard Sumit Kumar; an SIT has been formed.

articles.headline: 7 killed in twin blasts in Pakistan's Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
articles.summary: At least seven people were killed and three others injured on Saturday in twin roadside blasts in Pakistan’s Khyber Pakhtunkhwa. No group has claimed responsibility for the attacks yet, which occurred in the Marka Bera area of Bannu.

articles.headline: 1,700 evacuated from Caribbean resort after fire
articles.summary: A large fire almost completely destroyed a luxury resort in the Dominican Republic, forcing the evacuation of 1,700 tourists. Local media reported one Italian tourist died in the fire and several others required medical assistance.

articles.headline: Brazil: Millions get 'misanthropy' alert in civil defence system
articles.summary: Brazil is probing an intrusion into its civil defence alert system that sent messages containing the word 'misanthropy' to millions of mobile phones. The alert, normally for weather, was likely due to a hack.

articles.headline: 12 hurt after firing on Chicago crowd from SUV
articles.summary: At least 12 people in a Chicago crowd suffered wounds after an SUV pulled up and two people inside started shooting. The eight men and four women ranged in age from 17 to 47. Police are investigating the incident.

articles.headline: 1 dead, 80 hurt as two trains collide in UK
articles.summary: Nine people were critically injured and the driver of one train was killed after a collision between two passenger trains in the UK. Over 80 people were treated in hospitals, police said.

articles.headline: 1 dead after two cargo trains collide in Munich
articles.summary: One person was killed after two cargo trains collided on a bridge in Munich, Germany, on Saturday. Two of the carriages crashed onto the road below.

articles.headline: US intel warns Netanyahu will hinder Iran talks
articles.summary: US intelligence agencies warned the Trump administration that Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is likely to take steps that will undermine US-Iran negotiations. Netanyahu faces domestic political pressure to continue the war in Lebanon, despite ceasefire deals.

articles.headline: Iran resumes Kharg Island oil loadings after US blockade lift
articles.summary: Iran has resumed loading crude from its Kharg Island export terminal after a break of about six weeks, following the lifting of a US Navy blockade of its ports. Three very large crude carriers are moored at the terminal.

articles.headline: Australia detects H5 bird flu, virus now on every continent
articles.summary: Australia reported its first mainland case of H5 bird flu, found in a dead migratory seabird in remote Western Australia. This marks the spread of the deadly virus to every continent on the planet, though there's no evidence of poultry infections.

articles.headline: Trump's economy fears undercut US leverage in Iran talks
articles.summary: Donald Trump stated the prospect of global economic collapse was a big reason he signed an interim deal with Iran, easing sanctions on oil sales. This admission, coupled with Iran's subsequent closure of Hormuz, exposes a key US weakness in ongoing negotiations.

articles.headline: AAP protests against SAD for circulating 'fake' Mann video
articles.summary: The Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) on Saturday hit the streets across Punjab to denounce the Shiromani Akali Dal (SAD) for circulating a 'fake' video of Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann, which AAP claimed defamed him. AAP members called it a conspiracy.

articles.headline: EU lifts negative travel advisory for Assam, except AFSPA regions
articles.summary: Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma announced the European Union has lifted its negative travel advisory for the northeastern state, excluding AFSPA regions. He termed it a positive step to increase tourism and investment from 27 member-states.

articles.headline: Woman gets life term for killing husband in Tripura
articles.summary: A Tripura court has sentenced a 26-year-old woman to rigorous life imprisonment after convicting her of murdering her husband three years ago. Jhuma Marar was found guilty of strangulating Mohan Marar to death in Dhalai district.

articles.headline: 2 women gang-raped by 13 men in Bihar; SIT formed
articles.summary: Two women from a music and dance troupe were allegedly abducted and gang-raped at gunpoint by about a dozen men in Patna’s Naubatpur area. An SIT has been constituted to probe the matter, with three accused identified.

articles.headline: CISF gets legal powers in coal theft cases
articles.summary: The Central Industrial Security Force (CISF) has been granted direct enforcement powers in cases involving illegal coal mining and theft. An amendment to the MMDR Act transforms CISF into a statutory enforcement agency with authority to initiate legal action and conduct searches.

articles.headline: Lok Sabha Speaker Birla launches book on IITs
articles.summary: Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla released the book 'IIT - The story of India’s Most Prestigious Educational Ecosystem' at a PAN IIT Alumni India Function in New Delhi on Saturday. The book highlights the journey and impact of IITs.

articles.headline: PIL seeks judicial probe in Ayodhya temple row
articles.summary: A public interest litigation (PIL) was filed before the Lucknow bench of the Allahabad High Court seeking a high-level judicial commission to investigate allegations of embezzlement of donations at the Ram temple in Ayodhya. The hearing is likely next week.

articles.headline: Brazil back on track, but still seeking perfect rhythm
articles.summary: Brazil secured a comfortable 3-0 win against Haiti in the World Cup, with Matheus Cunha scoring a brace. Despite the victory, Brazil struggled to impose themselves until the final whistle, with concerns about Raphinha's injury and overall team cohesion remaining.

articles.headline: USA enters World Cup Round of 32 after easy victory
articles.summary: Co-hosts USA comfortably defeated Australia 2-0 to move into the Round of 32 with a game to spare. This marks their second consecutive World Cup victory. They benefited from an own goal for the second straight match, equaling Mexico for most own goals benefited.

articles.headline: Galarza scores World Cup's fastest winning goal
articles.summary: Matías Galarza’s goal for Paraguay, timed at 01:04, is the fastest winning goal in World Cup history since 2018. Paraguay beat Türkiye 1-0, despite Türkiye having 78.6% possession and 62 shots without scoring across two matches.

articles.headline: Brobbey, Gakpo double in Dutch World Cup dominance
articles.summary: The Netherlands achieved a dominant 5-1 victory over Sweden in a World Cup Group F match. Brian Brobbey and Cody Gakpo each scored two goals, with Summerville adding another late in the game.

articles.headline: Spain seeks Yamal spark to reignite World Cup campaign
articles.summary: European champions Spain flunked their opening World Cup test, drawing 0-0 against debutants Cape Verde. 18-year-old Lamine Yamal, nursed back from a hamstring injury, was kept in reserve but is expected to provide a crucial spark to reignite their campaign.

articles.headline: Neymar expected to return for Brazil against Scotland
articles.summary: Neymar is expected to return from a right calf injury and play for Brazil in its final World Cup group match against Scotland on Wednesday. Coach Carlo Ancelotti confirmed Neymar would go through individual training on Saturday and rejoin the team Monday.

articles.headline: Canada goalkeeper Crépeau resisted fan zone invitation to shoot
articles.summary: Canada goalkeeper Maxime Crépeau resisted fan calls to shoot during their World Cup victory, despite a 'devil here saying do it.' He delivered the ball upfield instead, later laughing about resisting the urge to shoot.

articles.headline: Madrid fan zone shut due to heat
articles.summary: A World Cup fan zone in Madrid, known as Plaza Seleccion, has been cancelled due to high temperatures of up to 39°C forecast. Despite Spain playing Saudi Arabia in an air-conditioned stadium, the outdoor event was called off for public safety.

articles.headline: Virat Kohli's fitness test on June 22
articles.summary: Virat Kohli is progressing well in his recovery from the right hamstring injury sustained during the IPL. He will report to the BCCI Centre of Excellence (CoE) on June 22 for re-evaluation.

articles.headline: Clark leads US Open by four strokes
articles.summary: Wyndham Clark, the 2023 US Open winner, curled in a 33-foot birdie putt at the 18th hole to fire a one-under par 69. This performance seized a four-stroke lead in Round 2 of the US Open at blustery Shinnecock, standing on seven-under 133 for 36 holes.

articles.headline: India faces big South Africa challenge in T20 World Cup
articles.summary: India's T20 World Cup campaign continues with two wins, but they face a significant challenge against South Africa at Old Trafford on Sunday. Spin bowler Shreyanka Patil has been ruled out due to an ankle injury, adding a complication for India.

articles.headline: Neeraj Chopra: Not fully fit yet, but happy with progress
articles.summary: Double Olympic and World Championships medallist Neeraj Chopra, back from an eight-month hiatus, expressed satisfaction with his comeback and progress in recovery from a back injury. He was named Best Athlete of 2025 at the AFI annual awards.

articles.headline: In Odisha, PM unveils ₹47,000-cr. infra push
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi unveiled development projects worth ₹47,000 crore for Odisha, focusing on power, roads, railways, health, and education. He also visited Pahadpur village, home to President Droupadi Murmu's in-laws.

articles.headline: NEET-UG retest to be held amid tight security
articles.summary: Over 22 lakh aspirants will take the NEET-UG re-examination today across 5,440 centers. The test was canceled over paper leak allegations. Stringent security measures, including CCTV and jammers, are in place.

articles.headline: One more Karnataka Bhavan to be set up in Delhi for students
articles.summary: Karnataka CM announced plans for Karnataka Bhavan-4 in New Delhi for civil services aspirants, costing ₹80 crore. It will provide housing and study space, with 50% accommodation reserved for SC/ST students.

articles.headline: 16 more DSTPs in pipeline to curb Yamuna pollution, govt. to award contracts by July
articles.summary: Delhi government plans to award contracts for 16 more Decentralized Sewage Treatment Plants (DSTPs) by July to clean the Yamuna. This aims to increase the city's sewage treatment capacity by 170 MGD.

articles.headline: Nine civic hospitals set to upgrade for quality of care
articles.summary: Nine municipal hospitals in Delhi will be upgraded under the Centre's 'Kayakalp' programme. The initiative focuses on improving critical care infrastructure, patient safety, and hygiene standards.

articles.headline: Shah launches temple corridor project in Kolhapur, says India witnessing cultural renaissance
articles.summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah launched a ₹500-crore temple corridor project in Kolhapur, calling it a cultural renaissance. He inaugurated development works at the Mahalaxmi temple.

articles.headline: Cong. opposes Goa circular on Yoga Day attendance
articles.summary: The Congress opposed a Goa government circular making Yoga Day attendance compulsory for students and teachers. They stated yoga is a personal choice and should not be an obligation.

articles.headline: RS election results won’t impact ruling alliance, says Jharkhand Congress
articles.summary: Jharkhand Congress stated Rajya Sabha poll results won't affect the ruling INDIA bloc and they will continue fighting the BJP. They blamed 'horse-trading' for their candidate's loss.

articles.headline: Odisha mob attack: four more held for assaulting two NGO workers; tally now 20
articles.summary: Four more individuals were arrested in Odisha for assaulting two NGO workers, bringing the total arrests to 20. The incident involved alleged child-lifters rumors and mob violence.

articles.headline: DMK govt. to blame for industries shifting to A.P.
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu Industries Minister S. Keerthana claimed two industrial houses that were interested in TN shifted to AP due to inordinate delays during the previous DMK government. She stated she has proof.

articles.headline: Telangana doctors oppose move to raise faculty retirement age
articles.summary: Telangana doctors' associations opposed proposals to increase the retirement age of medical faculty from 65 to 70, citing negative impacts on promotions, recruitment, and career opportunities for younger doctors.

articles.headline: Kerala HC directs police to safeguard inter-faith couple
articles.summary: The Kerala High Court directed police to safeguard an inter-faith couple as they travel to Madhya Pradesh to testify in a court case. The couple sought protection after receiving a threatening message.

articles.headline: Akhilesh will have to make way for Shivpal, says Rajbhar
articles.summary: SBSP chief Om Prakash Rajbhar claimed SP president Akhilesh Yadav may step aside for Shivpal Singh Yadav to lead the party, suggesting an impending SP split.

articles.headline: Revanth likely to raise SBI land row with Centre, RBI
articles.summary: Telangana CM A. Revanth Reddy is expected to raise the SBI land dispute with the Centre and RBI, suspecting a political dimension to the controversy and the bank's legal proceedings.

articles.headline: A.P. exporters upset over pesticide residue as China rejects chilli
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh exporters are concerned over pesticide residue in Indian chilli consignments rejected by China and urged the government to monitor farming to ensure residue-free crops.

articles.headline: Fertilizers in focus as Iran tightens Strait transit rules
articles.summary: India is focused on evacuating fertilizer carriers from the Persian Gulf due to Iran's Strait transit rules. Several Iranian-flagged vessels are being repatriated, with others being monitored.

articles.headline: Sintex plans to evolve into water management firm
articles.summary: Sintex BAPL, now part of Welspun Group, is redefining its identity as a water management solutions provider, expanding into water transportation and treatment, aiming for 5% of the ₹1.2 lakh crore market by 2030.

articles.headline: Lack of investment chapter in India-U.K. trade deal not a concern, says Muscatelli
articles.summary: Anton Muscatelli believes the India-U.K. trade deal's significance isn't hindered by the lack of an investment chapter, as trade and ease of doing business measures will drive FDI. However, the EU views it differently.

articles.headline: Shree set to boost defence HVAC output
articles.summary: Shree Refrigerations Ltd. inaugurated a new manufacturing facility in Karad, Maharashtra, to produce refrigeration and HVAC systems for the Indian Navy and defence sector, supporting 'Make in India'.

articles.headline: Human activity drives India’s ‘wild’ weather
articles.summary: A study found human activity is the primary driver of increased concentrated rainfall and floods in India. Scientists found evidence separating natural cycles from greenhouse gas forcing.

articles.headline: What a ‘super’ El Niño might mean for India’s monsoon
articles.summary: An El Niño event is strengthening, with odds favouring a "super" event by winter. Historically, strong El Niños have correlated with below-normal monsoons in India, impacting planning.

articles.headline: What does the U.S.-Iran agreement say?
articles.summary: The U.S.-Iran MoU calls for a ceasefire, respects sovereignty, and lifts U.S. naval blockade on Iran. Iran will downblend enriched material, while the U.S. won't impose new sanctions. Israel opposes the deal.

articles.headline: Why have India’s statistical databases been upgraded?
articles.summary: India upgraded its national accounts, IIP, CPI, and WPI databases to improve timeliness, representativeness, and accuracy. The changes address outdated bases and reflect current economic realities.

articles.headline: India meets SA in standing the test of time
articles.summary: India faces South Africa in a crucial Women's T20 World Cup match. Captain Harmanpreet Kaur will play her 200th T20I. India has had strong wins, but South Africa's recent series victory poses a challenge.

articles.headline: Neeraj, Parul win top honours at Indian Athletics Awards
articles.summary: Neeraj Chopra was named Athlete of the Year (men), and Parul Chaudhary won the women's award at the inaugural Indian Athletics Awards. The ministry also mandated an Athlete Well-being Policy.

articles.headline: Aryan makes the cut for CWG, Asian Games
articles.summary: Aryan Nehra clocked 7:59.36 to win the men's 800m freestyle gold at the National aquatics, bettering his own 'best Indian time'. He also secured the men's 50m butterfly crown.

articles.headline: Abhay-Velavan pair storms into Asian doubles final
articles.summary: India's Abhay Singh and Velavan Senthilkumar reached the Asian doubles squash final, set to defend their title. They defeated Malaysian third seeds in the semifinals.

articles.headline: Bringing life back to a thirsty land
articles.summary: Rayadurgam in Andhra Pradesh is witnessing an ecological revival through community-led water conservation and afforestation. Villagers and the Forest Department restored water bodies and planted saplings.

articles.headline: Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz Amid US Breach Claims and Lebanon Attacks
articles.summary: Iran announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, citing US breaches of commitment and Israeli attacks on Lebanon. A delegation heads to Switzerland for talks, while the US disputes the closure. Meanwhile, Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon killed at least 16, including children, amidst fragile truce talks.

articles.headline: Former MP Padamsinh Patil Acquitted in 2006 Nimbalkar Murder Case
articles.summary: A CBI court acquitted former MP Padamsinh Patil and seven others in the 2006 murder of Congress leader Pavanraje Nimbalkar and his driver. The court found the prosecution failed to establish charges and conspiracy beyond reasonable doubt.

articles.headline: NTA Clarifies Abu Dhabi NEET Centre Was Candidate's Voluntary Choice
articles.summary: The National Testing Agency stated a Nagpur candidate voluntarily selected Abu Dhabi for the NEET-UG re-examination via the correction window. NTA shifted the centre back to Nagpur after a request, citing a 'Student First' approach.

articles.headline: BJP Intensifies Punjab Outreach Ahead of Assembly Elections
articles.summary: BJP state in-charge Nitin Nabin began a three-day visit to Punjab to strengthen the party's organization and strategize for the 2027 Assembly elections, emphasizing an independent contest.

articles.headline: India Bans 16 Fixed-Dose Combination Drugs Lacking Therapeutic Justification
articles.summary: The government has prohibited the manufacture and sale of 16 fixed-dose combination drugs, including antibiotics and creams, citing a lack of therapeutic justification after a multi-year review. This aims to curb irrational medical pairings.

articles.headline: Trump Praises Modi as 'Tough Leader,' Respects Xi Jinping
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump described PM Narendra Modi as a 'great leader' and a 'tough cookie,' praising his ability to avoid wars. Trump also lauded Chinese President Xi Jinping and leaders of India, China, and Pakistan.

articles.headline: Meloni Hits Back at Trump Over G7 Photo Claims, Sparking Spat
articles.summary: Italian PM Giorgia Meloni dismissed Donald Trump's claims of begging for photos at the G7 summit, calling his 'unprovoked attacks senseless.' Trump had accused her of seeking attention and doing poorly in Italy.

articles.headline: Israel, Hezbollah Agree to Truce Amid US-Iran Talks
articles.summary: Israel and Hezbollah reportedly agreed to a truce in southern Lebanon, halting fighting that killed 47 Lebanese and four Israeli soldiers. This aims to support wider peace talks between the US and Iran.

articles.headline: India Sweeps Afghanistan 3-0 in ODI Series After Jaiswal's Century
articles.summary: Yashasvi Jaiswal scored an unbeaten 110 as India completed a 3-0 ODI series sweep against Afghanistan by nine wickets. Prasidh Krishna took 5 wickets, and Rohit Sharma scored 79.

articles.headline: India Advances to Hockey Women's Nations Cup Final After Dominant Win
articles.summary: India secured a spot in the FIH Hockey Women's Nations Cup final with a 6-0 victory over Chile. Navneet Kaur and Deepika scored twice, and India will face hosts New Zealand in the final.

articles.headline: Supreme Court to Hear Electoral Bonds and Environmental Protection Petitions
articles.summary: The Supreme Court is scheduled to hear petitions challenging the validity of electoral bonds and a critical case concerning environmental protection measures.

articles.headline: Jio Platforms Files DRHP for India's Largest-Ever IPO
articles.summary: Jio Platforms aims to raise ₹32,000-35,000 crore in India's largest IPO, primarily for debt repayment. This will be the Reliance Group's first consumer-focused IPO, led by Akash Ambani.

articles.headline: Turtlemint Fintech IPO Subscribed 45% on Day 1; Concerns Remain
articles.summary: Turtlemint Fintech Solutions' ₹883-crore IPO saw 45% subscription on day one. Concerns are raised about its unprofitability and valuation despite growth potential.

articles.headline: India Hosts BRICS NSA Meet on Global Security Challenges
articles.summary: India is hosting a BRICS National Security Advisers' conclave chaired by NSA Ajit Doval, discussing non-traditional security threats and regional conflicts. Chinese and Russian officials are attending.

articles.headline: Five Killed in Maharashtra Temple Roof Collapse; Inquiry Ordered
articles.summary: At least five people died and 18 were injured when the roof of an under-construction Hanuman temple collapsed in Parbhani, Maharashtra. Authorities have ordered a structural audit.

articles.headline: Hindus Protest in Bangladesh Over Alleged Religious Desecration
articles.summary: Protests have erupted in Bangladesh following alleged desecration of a picture of Lord Ram and threats to vandalize a temple idol. Hindus are condemning alleged government inaction.

articles.headline: Indian Ship Captain Charged in UK for Sanctions Breach
articles.summary: The Indian captain of a sanctioned Russian oil tanker was arrested and charged in the UK for breaching sanctions. His family has appealed for his release.

articles.headline: Karnataka Ready for Talks on Mekedatu Project with Tamil Nadu
articles.summary: Karnataka Minister R Ramalinga Reddy stated the state is ready for talks on the Mekedatu project with Tamil Nadu, asserting it won't reduce the Cauvery water share and will help regulate river flow.

articles.headline: Experts Advise on Planting Native Trees for Thriving Growth
articles.summary: Experts offer guidance on successfully planting native trees, emphasizing species selection, soil preparation, proper watering, and protection to ensure saplings thrive.

articles.headline: Treviso to Host Tiramisù Run Celebrating Dessert's Origins
articles.summary: Treviso, Italy, will host the Tiramisù Run on October 11, 2026, a 10K race culminating in unlimited tiramisu, celebrating the iconic dessert.

articles.headline: Morocco's Saibari Scores Twice, Fuels World Cup Dream
articles.summary: Ismael Saibari scored two goals, including the winner against Scotland, becoming the second African player to score in his first two World Cup appearances.

articles.headline: Paraguay's Almiron Red-Carded Under New FIFA Mouth-Covering Rule
articles.summary: Paraguay midfielder Miguel Almiron received the first red card under FIFA's new rule for covering his mouth during a confrontation, aimed at preventing racist comments.

articles.headline: AIFF Seeks Name Change to Football Federation of Bharat
articles.summary: The All India Football Federation (AIFF) decided to propose a name change to Football Federation of Bharat (FFB) to the sports ministry, seeking government and FIFA approval.

articles.headline: Husband Booked for Murder After Techie Found Dead in Mussoorie
articles.summary: Uttarakhand Police registered a murder case against the husband of a 27-year-old IT professional found dead at a Mussoorie homestay. Her father alleged foul play.

articles.headline: Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz Amid Escalating Israel-Lebanon Conflict
articles.summary: Iran shut the Strait of Hormuz, citing Israeli strikes in Lebanon that killed 20. US-Iran talks are scheduled for Sunday, with US intelligence suggesting potential continued Israeli attacks on Hezbollah.

articles.headline: Three Indian Supertankers Safely Cross Strait of Hormuz Before Closure
articles.summary: Three Indian-flagged crude oil supertankers transited the Strait of Hormuz safely before Iran's military declared it closed, carrying 94 Indian crew members.

articles.headline: IUML Exits DMK Alliance in Tamil Nadu Over TVK Association
articles.summary: The Indian Union Muslim League (IUML) has left the DMK-led alliance in Tamil Nadu after six decades, citing the post-poll association with Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK).

articles.headline: CID Inspector Among Three Held for Extortion in Bengaluru
articles.summary: A Karnataka CID inspector was arrested along with two others for allegedly assaulting men from Kerala and extorting ₹20 lakh at a Bengaluru lodge, impersonating police officers.

articles.headline: Akal Takht Releases Mann's Video Amid Deepfake Row
articles.summary: The Akal Takht released a video of Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann's January appearance, countering a row over an alleged objectionable video which Mann claims is a deepfake.

articles.headline: Ashmita Chaliha's Macau Open Run Ends in Semi-finals
articles.summary: India's Ashmita Chaliha lost to South Korea's Park Ga Eun 17-21, 9-21 in the Macau Open women's singles semi-finals.

articles.headline: Task Force to be Formed for F1 India Revival and Motorsports Growth
articles.summary: The Sports Ministry will establish a Task Force to assess the revival of Formula 1 in India and explore opportunities for broader motorsports development.
